sections.label: Lyrics
lines: Heaven’s throne is high and wide,
but You rest where the low hearts hide.
Not the towers, not the shine
You choose the cracks, the undersigned.
Every mountain bows in quiet,
every crown returns to clay.
Still You whisper through the ashes:
“I am near, don’t turn away.”
I brought rivers, I brought song,
I brought all that felt strong.
But You said, “Child, what I desire
is something small, something real.”
A humble heart, a contrite flame,
these You will not turn away.
Not the gold, not the altar’s art
You draw near to the broken heart.
When I fall, You don’t despise,
You lift my ruins to the skies.
O mercy, find me where I am,
make my spirit light again.
Micah’s road runs through the valley,
justice walking hand in hand
with mercy like a quiet river,
and truth that learns to stand.
Not the sacrifice of thunder,
not the noise of holy pride
just the love that does the small things,
and the faith that stays inside.
I have tried to build my temples,
stone by stone of my own will.
But the walls became my prisons,
till Your silence broke them still.
Now I see, it’s not perfection,
but surrender that You crown.
You raise the dust of every failure,
and breathe Your glory down.
Let my silence be a temple,
let my tears become the hymn.
Every breath an open window
where Your kindness enters in.
A humble heart, a contrite flame,
these You will not turn away.
Through the cracks Your glory starts
light is born in broken hearts.
You remake the dust with grace,
heaven’s fire in common clay.
O mercy, meet me where I am,
and make my spirit light again.
Still small voice, I hear You call
the higher road is low for all.
